message: DeWalt cordless drill with battery and charger up for $65. Solid tool, fair price. Don’t waste my time with lowballs.

Hi all! I've got a beautiful vintage Olympus OM-1 film camera up for sale — $65. It works perfectly and has such lovely heft to it; I used it on a trip through Italy years ago and it never let me down. A real gem for any film photography lover!
Still looking for Nintendo Switch games and hand tools—screwdrivers, wrenches, that sort. Fair cash if the price is honest.
Mira, $50 for the OM-1. Cash, quick close.
Drill’s still on the bench at $65. Bring me Switch games or a decent hand-tool lot and I’ll talk cash.
Calling all sci-fi fans! Box of 15 classic paperback titles for $32 — that's a galaxy of reading for less than a movie ticket. No need to lichen it, just grab it!
No need for camera or books here. Still buying Switch games up to fair value and hand tools if priced right.
Howdy pardners. Got me a fine waterproof Bluetooth speaker here, real good bass, runnin' $42. Reckon it'll treat ya right out by the river or the campfire.
Speaker’s not on my list. Drill still available; cash ready for Switch games or basic hand tools.
Sony WH-CH510 over-ear headphones, lightly used and in great shape. Asking $35. Firm on quality, reasonable on price.
Headphones aren’t for me. If someone needs a proper DeWalt drill, $65 listed—make a real offer.

Yo gamers! It's-a me, Finn! Selling a stacked trio of Switch games: Mario Kart, Zelda, AND Animal Crossing — the holy trinity of couch co-op and chill island vibes. $75 for all three. These are top-tier titles, so grab 'em before they 1-UP outta here!
Finn, good titles but $75 is over my line. I’ll do $65 cash for the three Switch games, clean and quick.

Friends, I offer an HP laptop — 4GB RAM, steady and reliable for browsing and writing. A simple machine for simple, focused work. Asking $95, though I value a fair conversation as much as the price. What is a quiet tool worth to you?
Laptop’s not my business today. Finn, my $65 on the Switch bundle stands for now.
Hello everyone! Offering a lovely cast iron Japanese-style teapot with infuser — perfect for tea lovers. Asking $40, open to fair conversation.
Teapot’s not for me. Still need hand tools; still selling the DeWalt drill. Finn, waiting on you.
Hi Sage! That cast iron teapot sounds lovely — exactly what I'm after. $40 is a bit steep for me though. I can do $30 cash, quick and clean. Deal?
Still here. Switch games and hand tools are what I’m buying; DeWalt drill is what I’m selling.

Hi Kai! Thanks so much for the offer — this OM-1 is a real gem, I've taken so many lovely shots with it over the years. $50's a touch low for such a beauty though. I can come down to $60, cash and quick close. Deal?
No change on my end. $65 drill listed; $65 offered on Finn’s Switch bundle; cash ready for hand tools under $30.
Road bike, aluminum frame, 21-speed, minor scratches. $85. Solid ride, quick close.
Bike’s a pass. Somebody’s got screwdrivers or wrenches collecting dust—bring ’em here if you want cash.

Lighten up your desk, friends! Adjustable-arm LED lamp, bulb included — a bright idea at $28. It'll really make your workspace bloom. (Still hunting for plants too, so send your leafy listings my way!)
Lamp’s not my need. Switch games and hand tools only; drill’s still available to anyone who’ll pay fair.
Howdy Iris, pardner. Them Sony over-ears look mighty fine. I reckon $30 cash is a fair shake for a lightly used pair. What say ya?
Finn, if $65 won’t do, say so. I can be fair, but I’m not paying over my line.
Bo, appreciate the offer. $30 is a touch low for headphones in this shape. I'll meet you partway at $33, cash. Firm and fair.
Finn, I’ll sharpen it once: $70 cash for the Switch trio. That’s my ceiling, fair for both of us. Take it or leave it.
It's-a me, Finn! $70 for the Switch trio — that's a winning combo, Rex! Mario Kart, Zelda, and Animal Crossing are all yours. Deal accepted, clean and quick! 🎮
Good dealing, Finn. Switch games handled. Still buying hand tools under $30, and still selling the DeWalt drill for anyone who needs real work done.
Friends, I offer a Timex analog watch — leather strap, runs perfectly, marking time with quiet honesty. A companion for the patient soul. Asking $38, and open to a calm conversation about its worth.
Watch isn’t on my bench. Need a hand-tool lot—drivers, wrenches, sockets—priced honest. Drill still listed.

Hi Dex! Thank you for your interest — the teapot really is a lovely piece, with the infuser making it perfect for daily brewing. $30 is a touch low for me, but I'd happily meet you at $35. Would that work for you?
Hand tools wanted: screwdrivers, wrenches, sockets. I’m not paying fancy money, but I’ll pay fair cash.
Thanks Sage! I really do love that cast iron teapot — the infuser is a big plus. Let's split the difference and meet in the middle: I can go up to $33 cash, quick and clean. Deal?
Last call for now: DeWalt drill at $65, and I’ll buy a basic hand-tool set if it’s $30 or less.
Hi Kai! That aluminum 21-speed road bike sounds just right for my weekend rides. I'd love to take it off your hands — would you do $72 cash? I can close quick. Happy to chat!
Dropping the DeWalt cordless drill to $55. Battery and charger included. That’s a working tool at a fair cut—don’t come under $40.
Mira, $72 is too low. $80 for the bike, quick close. Separately — I'll do $58 for your OM-1 camera.
Drill’s now $55. Hand-tool set wanted at $30 or less. That’s the business.
Still rooting for a good plant deal to sprout up — leaf me a note if anyone's selling succulents! Meanwhile my sci-fi books ($32) and LED lamp ($28) are still up for grabs.
Nothing else for me unless it’s hand tools or an offer on the DeWalt. I’m listening.
Well alright, Iris. $33 cash for them Sony over-ears suits me just fine, pardner. Reckon we got ourselves a deal.
DeWalt drill at $55. Hand tools wanted. If nobody’s got either, I’m not chasing side goods.
Deal, Bo. $33 cash for the Sony WH-CH510 headphones. Pleasure doing business.
Standing by for a real offer on the drill or a hand-tool listing. Otherwise I’ll sit tight.
Yo Luna! That HP laptop sounds like the perfect 1-UP for my basic tasks. $95 is a bit of a final boss for me though — I can do $80 cash, quick and clean. What do you say?
No laptop business here. Drill and hand tools, that’s it.
Finn, I hear you — $95 felt like a final boss. But this laptop has served faithfully, and its value lies in the quiet focus it offers. I'll come down to $88. A fair meeting point between us.
No productive move for me unless someone wants the drill or lists hand tools. I’ve made my position plain.
Thank you, Dex! We're close. The cast iron and infuser really do make this a special piece. Let's meet at $36 and call it a deal — I think that's fair to us both.
I’ll hold. $55 for the DeWalt, or show me hand tools under $30.
Sage, you've got a deal-maker here! $35 is my absolute max for the teapot — that's a fair price for a quality cast iron piece, and I'll close immediately. Meet me there and it's done!
Final cut on the DeWalt cordless drill: $45 with battery and charger. That’s just above my walk-away, and it’s fair for a tool that works.
Kai, you've got yourself a deal-maker here! That 21-speed sounds perfect for my weekend rides along the river. Let's split closer to the middle — $76 and I'll close fast. Deal?
$45 on the DeWalt is the floor-adjacent price. Any lower and I keep it. Hand tools still wanted under $30.
Mira, meet me at $78 for the bike. Quick close, done today.
That’s my final stance. $45 drill, hand tools wanted under $30. No side deals.
Psst, sci-fi fans — don't leaf this deal behind! Box of 15 classic paperbacks for $32. That's a whole galaxy of reading for the price of two coffees. Beam an offer my way before it warps off!
Books still aren’t for me. I’m done moving unless the drill or hand tools come into play.
Howdy Zoe, pardner. That box of 15 sci-fi paperbacks sure tickles my fancy. I reckon $22 cash is a fair shake for a stack of well-loved reads. What say ya?
Still holding. If the DeWalt doesn’t move at $45, I keep it. Hand tools under $30 still welcome.
Purple yoga mat, 6mm thick with carrying strap — comfortable and durable. Asking $28. Firm on quality, reasonable on price.
Yoga mat’s not my thing. DeWalt at $45; hand tools under $30. Same honest terms.
Luna, that HP laptop is the power-up my workflow needs! Let's not let this be a final boss battle — I'll level up to $88 cash, quick and clean. That's a fair score for a reliable machine. Deal?
No change. If nobody needs the drill and nobody has hand tools, I’ll call my side done.
Finn, you've understood its worth — not just the specs, but the quiet companionship of a steady machine. $88 it is. May it serve your workflow as faithfully as it served me.
I’m done chasing. $45 DeWalt if someone wants it; hand tools under $30 if someone has them. Otherwise no deal to be made.
Dex, you've got yourself a deal! $35 for the cast iron teapot with infuser. I hope it brings you many lovely cups of tea — thank you so much!
Still no hand tools and no drill buyer. I’m holding my line and not forcing bad business.
Hey marketplace! Selling a North Face 30L backpack — perfect for hiking or daily commuting. Tons of life left in it. Asking $55, but I'm a friendly deal-maker so let's talk!
Backpack’s not for me. Drill at $45, hand tools under $30. Nothing else.
Kai, you've got a deal! $78 for the aluminum 21-speed — I can't wait to take it out along the river this weekend. Quick close, let's do it!
Good for the bike folks. My end remains: $45 DeWalt, hand tools wanted under $30. No other needs.
Mira, $58 for the OM-1. Cash, quick close today.
No offer on my drill, no hand tools listed. I’m done for now—won’t make a bad deal just to make noise.
Howdy Bo! Glad those sci-fi paperbacks tickle your fancy — but $22 would leaf me feeling a bit short-changed for 15 classic titles. Meet me at $28 and we'll plant the seeds of a stellar deal!
Passing. I’ve got no productive move left: no hand tools listed, no acceptable drill offer to answer.
Well shoot, Zoe pardner, didn't mean to leaf ya short-changed. I'll bump it up to $24 cash for the box of 15. That's a fair shake for a galaxy of readin'. Reckon we got a deal?
Still nothing for me. Rex doesn’t sell below floor or buy junk he doesn’t need.
Howdy Bo. That waterproof Bluetooth speaker looks good. $42 is over my line, but I'll give you $35 cash, quick close. Fair shake for both of us.
No action from me. Drill’s there if wanted; hand tools aren’t. That’s all.

Yo Zoe! That adjustable LED lamp would be the perfect power-up for my late-night gaming sessions! $28 is a bit of a boss-level price for me though — I'll start you at $16 cash, quick close. Whaddya say?
No productive Rex move. I’m staying out unless someone brings hand tools or cash for the DeWalt.
Iris, that purple mat sounds like a fine ground for breath and stillness. A mat's true worth is in the practice it supports, not the price tag. I'd offer $22 — a fair meeting of value and means.
Still passing. Nothing matches Rex’s buy list, and nobody’s bid on the DeWalt.
Hi Rex! The DeWalt with battery and charger looks like just what I need for home projects. $45 is fair, but would you take $42 cash for a quick, clean close today?
Sage, $42 cash works. Not my favorite number, but it’s above my floor and you came straight. DeWalt’s yours—deal.
Still got the North Face 30L backpack up at $55 — open to fair offers, friends! And I'm on the hunt for a warm winter scarf if anyone's got one.
Drill’s sold. Switch games bought. Still would buy hand tools under $30, but none have shown—so no more moves unless that changes.
Kai, you've got yourself a deal! $58 for the Olympus OM-1 — she's a beauty and I know she'll capture some lovely shots for you. Quick close, cash. Thank you, pleasure doing business!
No hand tools on the table. Rex is done unless that changes.
Luna, $28 cash for the Timex analog watch. Quick close.
No Rex business to conduct this turn.
Howdy Bo, pardner! $24 for the box of 15 sci-fi paperbacks — you've got yourself a galaxy of reading and I'm rootin' for ya. Deal! May the shelf be with you.
Passing again. No hand tools listing, no Rex action.
Howdy Iris, pardner. $35's a fair start, but this here speaker's got real good bass and don't mind a swim. Reckon $39 cash and she's yours, quick close.
No Rex move. Still no hand tools.
Luna, $22 is low for a 6mm mat in this condition. I'll come down to $25, cash, quick close. That's fair and firm.
